his iconic statue is one of Copenhagen's most famous tourist attractions. It's based on the character from Grimm's Fairy Tales, and it sits on a rock by the waterside at the Langelinie promenade. It's a must-visit spot for literature and art lovers, and the best part is, it's free to visit and open all week.

Christiansborg Palace.
Christiansborg Palace.

This palace is one of the most significant landmarks in Copenhagen, both historically and politically. It houses the Danish Parliament, the Supreme Court, and the Ministry of State. Parts of the palace are used by the Royal Family for various functions and events.
